---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
pagetitle: "CV | Peleg Kremer"
---

::: {.button-row}
[Download full CV (PDF)](assets/Kremer_CV.pdf){.btn-quiet}
[About page](about.qmd){.btn-quiet .outline}
[Publications](publications.qmd){.btn-quiet .outline}
:::

::: {.lead}
A summary version of the CV is below. For the full document, including all conference presentations, posters, workshops, internal grants, and committee service, please download the PDF.
:::

## Education

- **PhD, Energy and Environmental Policy** · University of Delaware, 2011
- **M.A., Geography** · Tel Aviv University, 2005
- **B.Sc., Agricultural Economics** · Hebrew University of Jerusalem, 2000

## Academic appointments

- 2022 – present : Associate Professor, Department of Geography and the Environment, Villanova University
- 2021 – present : Program Director, Environmental Studies, Villanova University
- 2016 – 2022 : Assistant Professor, Department of Geography and the Environment, Villanova University
- 2015 – 2016 : Visiting Assistant Professor, Department of Geography and the Environment, Villanova University

## Selected funded research (current)

| Award | Funder | Role | Period |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| SCC-IRG Track 1: AI for GSI Sustainability | NSF | Co-PI | 2024 – 2028 |
| DISES: Mobility Equity Gaps from Nuisance Flooding | NSF | Co-PI | 2024 – 2028 |
| Beyond Land Precarity: Garden Database and Platform | William Penn Foundation | PI | 2025 – 2027 |
| Global Centers Track 2: Nature-based Urban Hydrology | NSF | Supporting | 2024 – 2025 |
| Heavy Metal Transport in GSI Sediments | Villanova GRASP | PI | 2026 – 2027 |

## Selected funded research (recent)

| Award | Funder | Role | Period |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Community Garden and Land Security in Philadelphia | William Penn Foundation | Co-PI | 2024 – 2025 |
| CDS&E: Revolutionizing Sustainable and Fair GSI through AI | NSF | Co-PI | 2022 – 2025 |
| Multi-Dimensional Structure of Urban Landscape and Ecosystem Services | NSF | PI | 2018 – 2024 |
| SCC-PG: Smart, Sustainable, Equitable GSI | NSF | Co-PI | 2022 – 2023 |

## Editorial roles

- Associate Editor, *Sustainable Cities and Society* (2025 – present)
- Lead Special Issue Editor, *Sustaining Cities under Environmental Extremes*, *Sustainable Cities and Society* (2024 – 2026)
- Editorial Review Board, *Frontiers in Environmental Science: Land Use Dynamics* (2018 – present)
- Lead Special Issue Co-Editor, *The Future of Urban Sustainability: Smart, Efficient, Green or Just?*, *Sustainable Cities and Society* (2016 – 2019)

## Selected awards

- Outstanding Faculty Mentor Teaching Award, Villanova University (2023)
- WRA Innovation and Collaboration – Research Award, Water Resources Association of the Delaware River Basin (2024)

## Selected service

- Senior Review Panel, NSF Human-Environment and Geographical Sciences (2024)
- Ad Hoc Reviewer, NSF HEGS (2026)
- Committee Chair, GEV Committee on Diversity and Inclusion (2016 – present)
- Member, Strategic Initiative for Climate, Justice, and Sustainability: Research Committee, Villanova (2024 – present)
- Member, CLAS DEI Committee (2020 – present)
- Long-standing partner, Philadelphia Garden Data Collaborative (2015 – present)

## Selected mentoring metrics

- 26+ graduate and undergraduate student researchers mentored since 2022
- Multiple advisees have first-authored peer-reviewed papers, including in *Urban Climate*, *Sustainable Cities and Society*, *Environmental Research: Infrastructure and Sustainability*, *Frontiers in Environmental Science*, and *Frontiers in Built Environment*
- See [Students](students.qmd) for the current list

## Professional memberships

American Association of Geographers · American Geophysical Union · Association for Environmental Studies and Sciences
